title: US Ebola Aid Plummets 99% Since Last Outbreak
summary: Former Executive Director of the USAID COVID-19 Task Force Jeremy Konyndyk joined Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. Konyndyk explains to hosts David Gura and Christina Ruffini the negative impact of the US withdrawal from the WHO in January and the dismantling of USAID on the public health response. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump’s 3,711 Trades Point to Multiple Stock-Market Strategies
summary: Index-tracking and automated execution appear to be on show in transactions linked to the president.

title: SpaceX Starship Deploys Mock Satellites in Successful Test
summary: SpaceX’s upgraded Starship successfully deployed mock satellites and returned to Earth largely unscathed, though its booster spun out of control and broke apart over the Gulf of Mexico. Bloomberg News Global Space Loren Grush joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bangladesh Central Bank Launches $5 Billion Fund to Spur Growth
summary: Bangladesh’s central bank unveiled a 600 billion-taka ($5 billion) package to boost economic growth, revive shuttered factories and support small businesses.

title: Gas Prices Rise as Summer Holiday Travel Begins
summary: Memorial Day weekend is expected to kick off one of the busiest summer travel season in years, with millions of Americans hitting the roads and airports despite rising costs. GasBuddy's Head of Petroleum Analysis Patrick De Hahn and The Points Guy Managing Editor Clint Henderson joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the climbing gas prices, expensive airfare and what travelers can expect this summer. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Uganda Confirms Three More Ebola Cases as Outbreak Spreads
summary: Uganda confirmed three new Ebola cases, including a health worker and a driver linked to the country’s first known infection, as authorities race to contain the outbreak. Bloomberg News Senior Editor Jason Gale joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Deadliest China Coal Mine Blast Since 2009 Kills at Least 90
summary: The death toll from a gas explosion at a coal mine in China’s Shanxi province has jumped to at least 90 people, making it the country’s deadliest such incident since 2009.

title: US and Iran Remain in Holding Pattern
summary: Bloomberg News White House Correspondent Kate Sullivan joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the most recent updates on Iran, including President Trump canceling his weekend plans to stay in DC. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Rubio Invites India’s Modi to Visit White House as Ties Improve
summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio met Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi in New Delhi on Saturday and invited him to visit the White House, as the two sides work to improve ties.

title: Japan's Akazawa has Brief Talk with China's Wang at APEC
summary: Japanese Trade Minister Ryosei Akazawa and Chinese Commerce Minister Wang Wentao spoke briefly Friday at an Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation dinner in Suzhou, China. The encounter marks the first face-to-face ministerial interaction between the nations since Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s comments on Taiwan strained ties. Akazawa confirmed the meeting at a press conference but declined to disclose what was discussed. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ebola Outruns Containment in Eastern Congo as Contact Tracing Falters
summary: Ebola is spreading faster than responders can track it in eastern Congo, where health workers managed to follow up with barely one in five identified contacts in a single day.

title: Charting the Global Economy: Factory Activity Sags on Inflation
summary: The global economy is showing signs of wearing down with inflation pressures persisting during the third month of a war-induced energy crunch.

title: ECB Hike May Be Inevitable to Keep Credibility, Stournaras Says
summary: Preserving the European Central Bank’s credibility is a strong argument in favor of an interest-rate increase next month, according to Governing Council member Yannis Stournaras.

title: Architect Norman Foster on Why the West Struggles to Build Big
summary: What it takes to make great engineering and design breakthroughs at scale.

title: Odd Lots: Norman Foster on the Struggle to Build Big (Podcast)
summary: Not many people think of designing buildings as an exercise in economics, but the entire process is defined by constraints around resources (both physical and financial), and an iconic building can also have a huge impact on the wealth and development of the area around it. So how do you encourage private developers to consider the public good when designing new projects? And how are some countries able to encourage more landmark building projects than others? In this episode, we speak with Norm

title: India Raises Diesel, Gasoline Prices for Third Time in Eight Days
summary: India’s state-run refiners raised retail prices again of diesel and gasoline on Saturday to help processors cut losses on discounted sales and to control a spike in demand.

title: Indonesia Plans to Beat Global Trading Giants at Their Own Game
summary: For years, Indonesia’s raw materials have been ferried from remote mines and plantations to global markets by armies of traders who handle negotiations, loans and even cranes and river barges.

title: New Zealand to Invest in Drones, Fleet to Shield Maritime Routes
summary: New Zealand intends to spend about NZ$1.6 billion ($936 million) on drones, ship maintenance and naval upgrades to bolster the island nation’s maritime security at a time of increasing concern about supply routes.

title: Abrego Garcia Wins Dismissal of US Human-Smuggling Case
summary: A federal judge in Tennessee dismissed human-smuggling charges against Salvadoran migrant Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia after concluding the US government brought the case in retaliation for his legal fight against being deported.

title: Cava CEO: Unique Cuisine Differentiates Us
summary: Cava CEO Bret Schulman discusses the success of his restaurant and just what distinguishes them from the competition. He talks about the Mediterranean cuisine that distinguishes his brand. He speaks with Romaine Bostick on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AEW CEO on WWE Challenger’s Biggest Test in New York
summary: All Elite Wrestling CEO Tony Khan discusses the future of AEW. He discusses his upcoming event, and how he plans to promote his league in the future. He speaks with Romaine Bostick on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Equatorial Leans Against Bidding on Copasa Privatization
summary: Equatorial SA is unlikely to bid on a key stake in Copasa, the Brazilian water utility that’s being privatized by the government of Minas Gerais state, according to people familiar to the matter.

title: Atomic Monster President on Business of Horror Films
summary: Atomic Monster President Michael Clear discusses the business of horror films and how they keep people going to theaters. He speaks with Romaine Bostick on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Space Capital CEO on What’s Next For Space Industry
summary: Space Capital Founder and CEO Chad Anderson discusses what’s next for the industry of space exploration. He speaks with Romaine Bostick and Isabelle Lee on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Grocery Stores Feel Crunch of Fuel, Labor Costs Ahead of Memorial Day
summary: Stew Leonard Jr., president and CEO of Stew Leonard's, said that customers are still buying food ahead of Memorial Day celebrations, but they're also complaining about the squeeze they feel when they go to the store. Leonard said that while he believes fuel prices will eventually come down once conflict in the Middle East is resolved, labor costs for the store will remain high, making it difficult to keep prices from going up.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Emerging Assets Post Positive Week as Hopes for Peace Deal Grow
summary: Emerging-market stocks and currencies gained on the week as investors grew increasingly hopeful that the US and Iran are moving closer to a peace deal.

title: Nasdaq Gets Approval From SEC to List Bitcoin Index Options
summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission has given the go-ahead for Nasdaq Inc. to list index options based on the price of Bitcoin, the latest sign that Wall Street is becoming more tightly integrated with the world of digital assets.

title: Lantheus Explores Potential $7 Billion Sale Following Offer From Curium
summary: Lantheus Holdings Inc. is weighing a sale after getting a takeover offer from Curium that values the radiopharma company at about $7 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: S&P 500 Posts Eighth Weekly Gain as AI Stocks Power Rally
summary: US stocks advanced, notching gains ahead of a long holiday weekend fueled by hopes for resolution of hostilities in the Middle East and relentless enthusiasm for artificial intelligence-linked trades.

title: Dollar Stalls as US-Iran Peace Hopes Boost Risk Sentiment
summary: The dollar ended the week nearly unchanged as risk assets got a boost tied to optimism around progress in peace talks between the US and Iran.
